iteration_1_pypdf_redirect.notes: Model tried built-in read tool on PDF (failed), and python -c with for loops (syntax error loop)

iteration_2_write_py_files.notes: Added instructions to write .py files instead of python -c. Merge failed on authors (hardcoded placeholders)

iteration_3_case_preservation.notes: Same skill as iter 2, re-run showed same scores

iteration_4_critical_case_note.notes: Added CRITICAL note about not lowercasing before extracting. Merge now 100 but text-extract varied due to model variance

iteration_5_two_step_workflow.notes: Tried two-step extract-then-parse workflow. Too complex for small model, scores regressed. Reverted to iteration 4 version.

changes_made: Redirected from nano-pdf CLI to pypdf for all read/merge/extract operations
Added explicit instruction to NOT use built-in read tool on PDFs
Added instruction to write .py files instead of python -c (fixes syntax errors with for loops)
Added complete code examples for extract, merge, page count, and JSON output
Added CRITICAL note about preserving case when extracting values (do not lowercase before extraction)
Added warning against hardcoding placeholder values
Defined clear workflow: pip install → write .py file → run → verify
key_findings: devstral-small gets stuck in infinite loops when nano-pdf CLI fails (GEMINI_API_KEY missing)
devstral-small cannot write Python for loops in python -c one-liners — must use .py files
devstral-small lowercases entire lines before extracting values, losing case of names
Text extraction task scores vary (53-100) due to model variance in parsing heuristics
Merge task is reliable (75-100) once proper instructions are provided
